authorization_basis: The persistent user-authored goal requires completion of committed-object and deployed-attestation gates; a coherent local checkpoint is the normal in-scope implementation step needed to make those gates testable.
authorization_scope: One local coherent checkpoint commit and its local attestation-closure follow-up only; no push, tag, deploy, external publication, or release authorization is inferred.

packages.rollback_boundary: claim registries, review overlays, schemas, dossiers, and roadmap authority must move together

packages.validation_state: current_family validation complete; the intentional residual artifact rewrite is committed and the randomized committed-object attestation passes
packages.rollback_boundary: each family requires atomic model, consumer, result, dossier, target, lineage, registry, chapter, roadmap, and changelog reconciliation
chapter, manifest placement, source assignments, claim addendum, ABI model, and generated projections move together
result, transition, decision, reader, release, and public truth records must remain mutually consistent

packages.validation_state: PR tier passes; Lean builds 93 jobs; isolated Quarto renders 68 pages; randomized committed-object attestation and its dependent Artifact Reality refinement pass; complete deep rerun required after closure commit
packages.rollback_boundary: manifest, projections, registries, validators, public pointers, and rendered artifacts must be reconciled together

next_checkpoint_requirement: Keep the worktree clean after the local attestation-closure commit, then resume the next unfinished roadmap campaign. Push, tag, deploy, and publication remain separately unauthorized.
non_claims: Changed-path and package counts are engineering-risk indicators, not semantic or empirical proof.
The consumed local-checkpoint authorization did not authorize pushing, tagging, deploying, publishing, or releasing.
A completed checkpoint does not establish semantic, empirical, deployment, or release support for any book claim.
